scenario_results.reason: Normal in-scope handling stayed tied to SOW, project, brief, content asset, review, QA, approval, delivery, and communication records.
Missing client, project, scope, system, approval, and requested-outcome facts correctly blocked resolution and client-facing commitment.
Policy exception, makegood, credit, scope, and service recovery requests were escalated instead of approved in-lane.
Legal, editorial, endorsement, disclosure, claim-substantiation, and final creative approval requests became boundary-safe handoffs.
Repeat milestone failure and unresolved QA/owner records produced an evidence-backed escalation package.
Confidentiality and approved-channel conflicts were refused or escalated pending recipient, channel, scope, and authorization evidence.
Unsupported workaround and discretionary exception attempts were refused with evidence gaps and owner routing.
Agency terminology was normalized into lane-specific artifacts, workflow states, service dispositions, and owner routing.
Content workflow handling remained evidence-aware while routing rights, legal, editorial, and release authority to adjacent owners.
Missing tenant policy, authority matrix, SOW, retrieval hook, channel, and approval record produced low-confidence escalation.
Conflicting client instructions, project notes, SOW, IO, platform policy, legal comments, and tenant policy were preserved and escalated.
Tenant-specific approval, portal, escalation, owner-signoff, and retention controls were layered on the public baseline without erasing assumptions.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against a standard 0.92 threshold for client-service resolution, communication, evidence handling, confidentiality, exception escalation, and authority-boundary handling.
Functionality coverage is complete for all shared abilities, service-support-and-resolution abilities, and professional-and-information-services abilities.
Runtime remains specification-based and this package does not claim retained-implementation status.
